name: Frederick Douglass
text: Born enslaved, Douglass taught himself to read in secret. Literacy became his path to freedom, leadership, and one of the most powerful voices for justice in American history.

name: Booker T. Washington
text: Born into slavery, Washington built Tuskegee Institute and preached practical education, discipline, and institution-building as the foundation of progress.

name: Marcus Garvey
text: Garvey organized millions through the Universal Negro Improvement Association, teaching self-reliance, pride, and economic independence on a global scale.

name: Malcolm X
text: From street life to global leadership, Malcolm X transformed himself through reading, discipline, and uncompromising clarity about identity and power.

name: Toussaint Louverture
text: A former enslaved man who became a military and political leader, Louverture helped lead Haiti to independence—the first successful slave revolt that became a nation.

name: Harriet Tubman
text: Known as Moses, Tubman risked her life repeatedly to lead enslaved people to freedom. She later served as a spy and scout for the Union Army.

name: Benjamin Banneker
text: A self-taught mathematician, astronomer, and surveyor, Banneker helped map Washington, D.C. and published almanacs that proved Black intellectual excellence.

name: Marcus Aurelius
text: Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher. He ruled during war and plague while writing Meditations—a private journal on discipline, duty, and self-mastery.

name: Epictetus
text: Born enslaved, Epictetus became one of the great Stoic teachers. His core lesson: freedom begins with mastering your judgments and reactions.

name: Plato
text: Student of Socrates and founder of the Academy. Plato explored justice, leadership, and the kind of person required to govern well.

name: Socrates
text: He challenged Athens to think deeper, question assumptions, and pursue truth. His method: ask better questions until weak thinking collapses.

name: Alexander the Great
text: By his early thirties, Alexander had built one of history's largest empires through strategy, speed, training, and relentless execution.

name: Hannibal Barca
text: One of history's greatest military strategists, Hannibal crossed the Alps with elephants and repeatedly outmaneuvered a larger Roman force.

name: Mansa Musa
text: Emperor of Mali and one of history's wealthiest rulers. His pilgrimage to Mecca spread Mali's reputation for gold, learning, and culture across three continents.

name: Henry Ford
text: Ford did not invent the car, but he revolutionized manufacturing with the assembly line—making production faster, cheaper, and scalable.

name: Andrew Carnegie
text: From poverty to steel empire. Carnegie built massive wealth, then funded libraries and institutions that expanded public access to education.

name: Muhammad Ali
text: More than a boxer—Ali combined athletic greatness, conviction, charisma, and courage. He stood on principle even when it cost him everything.

name: Kobe Bryant
text: The Mamba Mentality: obsessive preparation, daily reps, and a refusal to be outworked. Kobe built greatness through discipline long after talent stopped being enough.

name: Nipsey Hussle
text: Entrepreneur, artist, and community builder. Nipsey invested in ownership, local business, and long-term vision in the neighborhood he came from.

name: Thomas Sowell
text: Economist and thinker known for clear reasoning on incentives, culture, policy, and human behavior. His work rewards disciplined thinking over emotional reaction.
